title: Strict structured outputs (tool_choice / JSON schema) for routing and plan parsing
source: Anthropic tool use forced tool_choice + strict input schemas — docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/build-with-claude/tool-use; live fetch blocked this run
description: smart_select_agent (delegate.py) and the sprint planner (orchestrate.py) rely on prompting the model to 'Respond with ONLY {json}' and then string/regex-parsing the reply. This is brittle: prose leakage or markdown fences break parsing and silently fall back to keyword heuristics. Forcing a defined tool with a strict JSON schema guarantees schema-valid output and eliminates the fallback path for well-formed responses.
proposed_changes: Define JSON Schemas in schemas.py for the routing decision ({agent, complexity}) and the sprint plan ({tickets:[...]}). Add a helper that invokes claude with a single tool whose input_schema is the schema and tool_choice forcing that tool (SDK path) or `--output-format json` with post-validation against the schema (CLI path). Replace the ad-hoc parsing in smart_select_agent and the plan parser with schema validation; keep the keyword fallback only for transport errors, not for parse failures.
